paper.title: A Detailed Chemical Study of the Extreme Velocity Stars in the Galaxy

extraction.summary: Extracted 3 HVS candidates from 2407.02593: star 7 (definitively unbound), star 5 (marginally bound/possibly unbound), and star 8 (could be unbound/marginally unbound). The remaining 13 stars from the sample of 16 are explicitly concluded to be bound to the Galaxy and were excluded.

method_chain.summary: Gaia DR3 astrometry, photometry, and proper motions
outputs: RA, Dec, proper motions, G-band photometry

source_refs.context: The astrometry is from Gaia DR3

method_chain.summary: Selection of 16 candidate late-type HVSs from literature catalogs (Bromley_2018, Hattori_2018a, Marchetti_2019b) and ADQL queries on Gaia DR2/DR3

source_refs.context: Target selection from literature and ADQL queries

method_chain.summary: Ground-based high-resolution spectroscopic follow-up at Apache Point Observatory (ARCES) and McDonald Observatory (Tull Echelle Spectrograph) to measure radial velocities

source_refs.context: Observations using Tull Echelle Spectrograph and ARCES

method_chain.summary: Adopted photo-geometric distance estimates from Bailer-Jones_2021 in lieu of Gaia parallax inversion because more than a third of stars have relative parallax errors >10%

Atmospheric parameter and abundance determination using spectral synthesis with LTE models in a Bayesian framework (LoneStar code). Combined spectroscopic, photometric, and astrometric constraints via isochrone fitting.

Calculation of total velocity v_total and galactocentric distance r using Gaia DR3 astrometry, Bailer-Jones_2021 distances, and ground-based RVs. Monte Carlo sampling of 1000 realizations for uncertainty propagation.
Milky Way gravitational potential model from Williams_2017, spherical model with 1-sigma parameter uncertainties
Comparison of v_total to escape velocity curve to determine bound/unbound status. Monte Carlo sampling propagates astrometric, RV, and distance uncertainties.
Orbit integration using galpy backwards in time to search for progenitor systems (globular clusters and Milky Way satellites). Integration lengths of 300 Myr to 1.5 Gyr explored.
Assessment of possible origins by comparing chemical abundances and dynamics to globular clusters, satellite galaxies, LMC, and stellar halo populations

source_refs.context: Possible origins for unbound or marginally bound stars

inclusion_assessment.summary: Paper concludes star 5 is marginally bound (1-sigma level) based on the Williams_2017 escape velocity model. It is one of three stars discussed as possibly unbound or marginally bound.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: The paper explicitly discusses star 5 as marginally bound at the 1-sigma level in multiple places (Figure caption, Results, Discussion, and Summary).

citation_context_refs.context: Reference column indicates Marchetti_2019b as source

teff.description: Spectro-photometric fit combining LTE synthesis with isochrone constraints

other.name: origin_assessment_summary
other.raw_value: No clear dynamical progenitor found. Closest approach is Draco II at 3.5 kpc. Chemical abundances consistent with stellar halo. Paper concludes likely born in situ stellar halo.
other.description: No clear dynamical progenitor found. Closest approach is Draco II at 3.5 kpc. Chemical abundances consistent with stellar halo. Paper concludes likely born in situ stellar halo.

inclusion_assessment.summary: Paper concludes star 7 is definitively unbound from the Galaxy based on the Williams_2017 escape velocity model. It is the only star in the sample classified as very likely unbound.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: The paper explicitly states star 7 is 'definitively unbound' and 'very likely unbound' in multiple places (Figure caption, Results, Discussion, and Summary).

citation_context_refs.context: Reference column indicates Bromley_2018 as source

teff.description: Spectro-photometric fit combining LTE synthesis with isochrone constraints

other.name: origin_assessment_summary
other.raw_value: Previously characterized in Bromley_2018 and Du_2018. No clear dynamical progenitor found. Closest GC approach is NGC 6205 at ~1 kpc (1.5 Gyr integration). Chemical abundances consistent with stellar halo ([alpha/Fe] ~ 0.4). Paper concludes likely born in situ stellar halo, ruling out LMC and accreted origins.
other.description: Previously characterized in Bromley_2018 and Du_2018. No clear dynamical progenitor found. Closest GC approach is NGC 6205 at ~1 kpc (1.5 Gyr integration). Chemical abundances consistent with stellar halo ([alpha/Fe] ~ 0.4). Paper concludes likely born in situ stellar halo, ruling out LMC and accreted origins.

inclusion_assessment.summary: Paper concludes star 8 could be unbound based on the overlap between its v_total uncertainties and the escape velocity uncertainties. It is discussed as marginally unbound (1-sigma level) or potentially marginally bound.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: The paper explicitly discusses star 8 as 'could be unbound' and 'marginally unbound' in multiple places (Figure caption, Results, Discussion, and Summary).

citation_context_refs.context: Reference column indicates Bromley_2018 as source

teff.description: Spectro-photometric fit combining LTE synthesis with isochrone constraints

other.name: origin_assessment_summary
other.raw_value: No clear dynamical progenitor found. With modified potential (heavier DM halo), closest approach is NGC 5897 at ~280 pc ~1.25 Gyr ago. Chemical abundances consistent with stellar halo. Paper concludes likely from stellar halo.
other.description: No clear dynamical progenitor found. With modified potential (heavier DM halo), closest approach is NGC 5897 at ~280 pc ~1.25 Gyr ago. Chemical abundances consistent with stellar halo. Paper concludes likely from stellar halo.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The 13 stars from Table 1 (tab:obs) that the paper concludes are gravitationally bound to the Galaxy
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper explicitly concludes these 13 high-velocity stars are bound to the Galaxy, despite having total velocities v_total > 300 km/s (all but one > 350 km/s). Only stars 5, 7, and 8 are discussed as possibly unbound or marginally bound.

source_refs.context: Summary stating the remainder are bound
